title: Smart Power System Monitoring with ML
description: IoT-based power monitoring system using ESP32 and PZEM-004. Architected a full data pipeline: Node-RED as orchestrator → Prometheus time-series DB → Grafana dashboards. Integrated an ML model for automated electrical load classification.

title: Home Assistant — Embedded & AI Hub
description: Full local-cloud smart home system with Raspberry Pi as the core controller and ESP32 edge nodes. Integrated WISPR AI for high-accuracy voice-command control of appliances and built an ML model for predictive monthly electricity bill estimation.

title: Computer Vision Card Game (AR Math-Puzzle)
description: Real-time AR math puzzle using a CV pipeline with HSV masking, perspective warping, and template matching. A multi-threaded C++ engine synchronizes live physical card data as mathematical operators with sub-millisecond latency.

title: LED Matrix Handheld Game
description: Handheld game on ESP32 with dual-core task distribution for zero-latency gameplay. Uses low-level GPIO register manipulation and NVS for persistent high-score tracking — built on bare ESP-IDF with FreeRTOS.

title: Visual Servoing on Quadruped Robot
description: Implemented Image-Based Visual Servoing (IBVS) for autonomous position adjustment of a quadruped robot. Achieved precise alignment and distance control for robust autonomous grasping using real-time camera feedback.

title: DSA Boss Fight Game
description: C++ game built as a practical study of data structures. Implemented an Object Pooling Pattern to eliminate runtime memory allocation overhead and optimized CPU cache utilization via contiguous memory structures for complex AI enemy behaviors.

title: Wall-Avoider Mobile Robot
description: Autonomous mobile robot using five ultrasonic sensors for 360° real-time obstacle avoidance. Engineered a differential drive locomotion model for seamless navigation in confined spaces.

title: IoT Distance Target Game
description: Edge-computing game where an ESP32 handles real-time distance tracking and game logic. Firmware interfaces ultrasonic sensors with a Node.js/Express backend via low-latency data transmission.

title: Hardware FSM — 7-Segment Display
description: Hardware-based Finite State Machine using 74LS ICs and JK flip-flops displaying a synchronized two-digit sequence (17→37→57→77). Boolean optimization for minimal state transitions; NE555 timer for adjustable clock speed.

title: Automatic Sorting Robot ARM
description: Autonomous sorting system using a robot arm with ultrasonic and TCS color sensors for real-time object classification and pick-and-place. Programmed complete detection, classification, and actuation pipeline.
